About: It is about dogs competing in jumping for distance or height from a dock into a body of water.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with the Incredible Dog Challenge.
It is associated with DockDogs.
It is associated with the Super Retriever Series.
It is associated with Splash Dogs.
It is associated with Ultimate Air Dogs.
It is associated with the United Kennel Club.
It is associated with North American Diving Dogs.
It is associated with the American Kennel Club.
It is associated with the Canadian Kennel Club.
It is associated with Dash 'n' Splash.
ConsistsOf: It consists of dogs jumping for distance or height from a dock into a body of water.
FoundedBy: It was founded by Milt Wilcox.
It was founded by Debra Markwardt.
HasA: It has events in the United States and other countries.
It has disciplines like Extreme Vertical, Speed Retrieve, and Iron Dog.
It has disciplines like Hydro Dash and Air Retrieve.
It has techniques called Place and send and Chase.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by dogs competing in jumping for distance or height.
It is characterized by a dock from which dogs jump.
It is characterized by a body of water into which dogs jump.
It is characterized by various organizations that run competitions.
It is characterized by specific measurement techniques for jump distance.
It is characterized by different disciplines.
It is characterized by specific techniques to encourage jumping.
It is characterized by divisions for ranking teams.
It is characterized by notable competitions.
HeldIn: It was held in the United States.
It was held in the United Kingdom.
It was held in southern England.
It was held in all of England and Scotland.
IsA: It is a timed run, jump, and swim.
KnownAs: It is known as dock diving.
KnownFor: It is known for distance jumping.
It is known for height jumping.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Incredible Dog Challenge.
ProducedBy: It was produced by pet food manufacturer Purina.
UsedFor: It is used for distance jumping.
It is used for height jumping.
It is used for qualifiers for ESPN Great Outdoor Games.
It is used for titles through the United Kennel Club.
It is used for recognized UKC sport.
It is used for diving dog titles recognized by the American Kennel Club and Canadian Kennel Club.
It is used for retrieving toys.
It is used for timed runs, jumps, and swims.
It is used for a triathlon combining disciplines.
UsedIn: It is used in the United States.
It is used in the United Kingdom.
It is used in North American Diving Dogs competitions.
UsedTo: It is used to measure jump distance.
It is used to rank teams.
It is used to encourage dogs to jump into the water.
